results.skills: finance

results.public_job_desc:
The Events Manager supports the full event marketing process — from planning and logistics to execution, tracking, and post-event reporting. This role plays a key part in driving operational excellence and administrative efficiency for the T-Mobile for Government marketing team. Responsibilities include managing event logistics, Salesforce campaign setup, financial tracking, and reporting — ensuring events are executed seamlessly, on time, and within budget. The Events Manager collaborates cross-functionally with internal stakeholders, external agencies, and senior leadership to deliver events that align with T-Mobile for Government’s brand, business, and communication objectives.
Technology Requirements
• Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, PowerPoint, Word)
• CRM Software (Salesforce Campaign Management)
• Communication Platforms (Teams, Zoom, Slack)
What Does a Typical Day Look Like?
Planning and Strategy
• Support development of event plans, calendars, and tracking documents.
• Assist in gathering data from previous events and compiling performance summaries.
• Coordinate campaign creation in Salesforce to track event leads, contacts, and ROI.
• Align event details with business goals and marketing objectives.
Cross-Functional Collaboration
• Partner with internal departments (Sales, Legal, Procurement, Finance) to ensure event alignment and compliance.
• Manage communication with external vendors and agencies to coordinate logistics, creative assets, and production.
• Support leadership with regular updates and ensure visibility across internal stakeholders.
Creative Development
• Contribute to creative and experiential ideas that align with the brand.
• Assist in the review and approval process for creative assets and collateral.
• Maintain brand consistency across all event materials and touchpoints.
Execution Oversight
• Manage event logistics including vendor coordination, shipping, registration, and on-site support.
• Track deliverables, deadlines, and task completion using shared project management tools.
• Support on-site event setup and execution as needed.
Administrative & Operational Support
• Create and manage Salesforce campaigns for all events, ensuring data accuracy and lead flow to Sales.
• Manage accounts payable processes: initiate POs, track invoices, and reconcile expenses.
• Maintain and track event budgets, processing monthly updates and reporting variances.
• Use Excel to develop trackers, dashboards, and financial reconciliations.
• Support quarterly and annual reporting on event ROI, lead generation, and budget utilization.
Communication and Reporting
• Draft and distribute internal communications regarding upcoming events, deadlines, and deliverables.
• Compile and deliver post-event recaps with key metrics, outcomes, and recommendations.
• Maintain internal reporting tools and shared drives for event documentation and tracking.
Lead Generation Focus
• Partner with Sales and Marketing Operations to ensure effective lead capture and follow-up workflows.
• Track Salesforce data integrity and campaign performance for accurate event ROI measurement.
Budget Management
• Manage event budgets and maintain spend tracking documentation.
• Support cost optimization by evaluating vendor proposals and identifying efficiencies.
• Reconcile post-event financials and ensure timely invoice processing and reporting.
Preferred Background / Prior Work Experience
Event Operations & Marketing Experience
• Demonstrated experience supporting the planning and execution of corporate or government-focused events.
• Familiarity with both internal and external event programs (tradeshows, conferences, sponsorships).
Administrative & Financial Expertise
• Experience with budget tracking, vendor management, and accounts payable workflows.
• Working knowledge of Salesforce and Microsoft Excel for reporting and campaign tracking.
Cross-Functional Collaboration
• Proven ability to work across internal departments and with external agencies.
• Experience coordinating with Finance, Procurement, and Sales Operations teams.
Metrics-Driven and Process-Oriented
• Strong analytical skills to interpret data and assess event ROI.
• Ability to maintain organized systems and optimize processes for efficiency.
Project Management
• Detail-oriented with strong task management and prioritization skills.
• Experience managing multiple projects simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.
Priority Soft Skills
• Communication Skills: Clear, concise, and effective in both written and verbal communication.
• Organization & Accountability: Excellent follow-through with the ability to manage competing priorities.
• Adaptability: Comfortable in dynamic environments and able to pivot quickly as priorities evolve.
• Analytical Thinking: Proficient with Excel and data-driven decision making.
• Collaboration: Builds strong relationships and fosters teamwork.
• Problem Solving: Proactive approach to identifying and resolving challenges efficiently.

results.public_job_desc:
Job Title: People Operations Coordinator 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Remote 

Duration: 6 Months  

  

Job Overview: 

We are searching for a collaborative individual who empathizes with the needs of employees and is proficient in resolving obstacles that may emerge. You have a steadfast dedication for delivering excellent service and are an expert in effectively prioritizing tasks while maintaining quality and attention to detail. 
 
In this role, you'll contribute to the company’s success by delivering a best-in-class support experience to employees. You'll tackle queries across all functional areas - HR, Compensation, Benefits, Workforce Management, and more - while ensuring our intranet remains updated. Your involvement will be pivotal in establishing scalable processes for employee support, playing a crucial role in our ongoing success. 
  

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Partner with stakeholders from all employee support teams (Payroll, Benefits, Compensation, Talent Success, IT, Legal, etc.) to assist in answering a broad range of employee questions using a shared services support model 

  • Serve as the first point of contact for employee inquiries in ServiceNow, responding to and resolving cases within SLA on a variety of topics (onboarding, benefits, time off, payroll, company policies, Workday support, offboarding) 

  • Maintain cases in your ServiceNow queue and effectively manage communications with employees, meeting case closure and SLA adherence goals 

  • Prepare and complete time-sensitive documents, forms, letters, and paperwork accurately and route them to appropriate teams for further processing in a timely manner (this includes I-9s, personnel files, employment verifications, unemployment claims, and offboarding paperwork) 

  • Complete weekly and monthly audits with a strong level of accuracy and attention to detail 

  • Apply the highest level of confidentiality regarding sensitive information and employee records 

  • Demonstrate proven ability to effectively partner with other teams and stakeholders 

  • Be champion of the Employee Service Center model by guiding employees to our company intranet and AI-powered agent for self-service, identifying gaps in self-service content and providing suggestions as needed 

  • Perform other duties or special projects of a similar nature as assigned  

  

Job Requirements: 

  • 1+ years of HR-related experience (could include HR exposure through classes or coursework) 

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills - you can deliver both good and bad news in a professional, compassionate manner 

  • Outstanding problem-solving skills and a strong focus on customer satisfaction 

  • An eye for process improvement - you leave it better than you found it 

  • Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment while quickly adapting to evolving processes, policies, and technological advancements 

  • Self-starter who can work reliably in a remote setting 

  • High attention to detail and the ability to maintain flawless accuracy 

  • Understanding of HR processes and systems, including ServiceNow HRSD and Workday (preferred) 

  

*Salary Range: $25/hr with Medical, Vision, and Dental Benefits

results.public_job_desc:
Job Title: People Operations Coordinator 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Remote 

Duration: 6 Months  

  

Job Overview: 

As an Employee Service Coordinator, you will be responsible for executing and supporting HR operations and transactions through Workday, ServiceNow HRSD, and other internal systems. Your day to day will focus on driving data accuracy, process consistency, and timely resolution of employee inquiries. You'll be a key player in enabling seamless onboarding, organizational changes, and recurring HR programs. 

  

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Support and process HR transactions as part of a tiered service delivery model, partnering with Tier 1 and various cross functional teams 

  • Monitor & maintain the Employee Service Center ServiceNow queue by resolving Tier 2 tickets, meeting case closure, and SLA adherence goals 

  • Manage a high volume of HR transactional work related, but not limited to hires, job changes, reorganizations, compensation changes 

  • Ensure data accuracy and integrity when entering various employee lifecycle changes in Workday 

  • Complete routine audits with a strong level of accuracy and attention to detail 

  • Apply the highest level of confidentiality regarding sensitive information and employee and candidate records 

  • Perform other duties or projects of a similar nature as assigned  

  

Job Requirements: 

  • 2+ year of experience working in Workday(required), performing a wide range of employee lifecycle transactions 

  • Detail oriented and highly organized, with a consistent track record of delivering accurate work 

  • Self motivated, can work reliably in a remote setting, and balances independent work while collaborating effectively with cross functional teams 

  • Comfortable handling multiple requests and adjusting when priorities shift 

  • Demonstrated ability to quickly learn and adapt to new systems, tools, and workflows 

  • A team player who actively looks for ways to improve processes and support peers 

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills 

  • Ability to provide clear and professional service to various stakeholders 

  • Discretion and the ability to manage confidential information with integrity 

  • Prior Experience in HR operations, shared services or employee support 

  • Familiarity with ticketing systems and SLA driven support models is a plus 

  

*Salary Range: $25/hr with Medical, Vision, and Dental Benefits

results.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Recruiting Coordinator 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Onsite (Seattle, WA) 

Duration: 6 Months  

  

Job Overview: 

Our Operations Recruiting Coordinator (RC) works closely with a team of recruiters in a fast-paced recruiting organization. The RC is an integral partner in managing recruiting activities and building candidate relationships; often providing candidates with their first impression of the company. This candidate will be an organizational guru, work effectively with internal and external clients and candidates, possess great communication skills, have the ability to shift gears at a moment’s notice and enjoy the challenges of helping to build a great organization of talented individuals. This is an amazing opportunity for someone looking to broaden their experience and work in a top recruiting organization. 

  

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Scheduling interviews and phone screens working directly with candidates and hiring managers, in some cases arranging travel for visiting candidates 

  • Updating candidate records and job postings in recruiting systems and confirming new hires 

  • Providing follow-up correspondence to candidates on recruiting status via phone and email 

  • Tracking recruiting activities and providing candidate status in a weekly report 

  • Coordinating the post-interview debrief meetings and providing debrief materials 

  • Identifying opportunities for improving candidate experience and scheduling efficiency 

  • Assisting in the coordination of other recruiting activities as needed 

  • May be required to work weekend shifts  

  

Job Requirements: 

  • Bachelor’s degree 

  • 1+ years of recruiting coordinator or coordination experience 

  • Experience with MS Word, Excel and Power Point  

  • Superb customer focus, attention to detail and organization skills 

  • Strong oral and written communication skills 

  • Ability to work in a fast paced, quickly changing environment 

  

*Salary Range: $22/hr with Medical, Vision, and Dental Benefits

results.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Executive Assistant I 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Onsite (Bellevue, WA) 

Duration: 3 Months  

  

Job Overview: 

We are looking for an Administrative Assistant who wants to work in a fast-paced, exciting, and growing organization. We are seeking an exceptionally talented, dependable, customer-obsessed, and self-motivated Administrative Assistant to work under the direct supervision of the Executive Assistant to the Vice President of our Transportation department.   

  

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Back up of calendar and support and scheduling needs for leaders on the department 

  • Support logistics and planning for leadership meetings and support in managing events 

  • Coordinate travel and create detailed itineraries 

  • Complete and file expense reports 

  • Tracking and helping drive completion of deliverables and following up on open action items 

  • Special projects as assigned  

  

Job Requirements: 

  • High school or equivalent; Bachelor's degree preferred 

  • 1+ years of working with computers and Microsoft Office products and applications experience 

  • Experience with end-to-end project management 

  • Experience with domestic or international travel coordination 

  • Demonstrated ability to design and improve processes 

  • 1 year+ experience with Executive Level Calendar Management 

  • 1 year+ experience planning, organizing, and supporting large meetings and events 

  • Strong organizational, communication, and interpersonal skills 

  • Ability to prioritize and handle multiple assignments at any given time 

  • High level of integrity and discretion 

  • Ability to work effectively with minimal supervision 

  • Superior attention to detail and ability to meet tight deadlines 

  • Ability to complete complex tasks quickly with little to no guidance and react with appropriate urgency to situations that require a quick turnaround 

  

*Salary Range: $31/hr with Medical, Vision, and Dental Benefits

results.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Program Coordinator 4 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Remote 

Duration: 3 Months 15 Days  

  

Job Overview: 

This role will contribute to providing additional support to drive and define the structure for this new program. The role will identify gaps in current processes and structure, and build out this structure to work better. The role will send out status updates to various teams, ensure tasks are defined, timelines are met, and work is driven forwards by controlling and tracking various moving pieces. The role may also put together high level decks that describe processes to supplement the current decks that exist. 

  

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Oversees the time recording and resource demands made by project/program teams, ensuring that it is recorded in an accurate and meaningful way 

  • Provide full range of project and resourcing support on selected projects and programs 

  • Co-ordinate and maintain Management Information (MI) which is timely, accurate and communicated effectively 

  • Ensure projects and programs operate within the defined Project Governance controls and meet the required recording standards 

  • Provide financial and resource planning and management support to projects and programs 

  • Planning as required in support of project and program governance (eg. Project plans; risk and issues logs, etc) 

  • Help develop and maintain resource model estimates and tracking / forecasting resource movements and spend 

  • Co-ordinate, and where appropriate lead various forums / boards / meetings in support of project/program managers 

  • Trains and supports colleagues on process and tools usage 

  

Job Requirements: 

  • Bachelor’s degree required  

  • 6+ overall years of experience as a program coordinator, or 2+ years as a project/program manager 

  • At least 6 years of experience with program organizational skills 

  • At least 2 years of project planning and tracking experience 

  • Strong analytical skills for interpreting data 

  • Prior experience as a Microsoft Corp. Contractor 

  • Previous knowledge and experience of project management methodologies 

  • Experience with MS Office programs and project management tools  

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills 

  • Strong analytical skills for interpreting data 

  • Very strong organizational skills 

  • Ability to remain flexible in response to changing priorities 

  • Self-motivated and pro-active 

  

*Salary Range: $33/hr with Medical, Vision, and Dental Benefits

results.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Project/Program Manager III 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Onsite (Seattle, WA) 

Duration: 6 Months  

   

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Responsible for the coordination and completion of project/program 

  • Oversee all aspects of projects/program 

  • Set deadlines, assign responsibilities, and monitor and summarize progress of project/program 

  • Prepare reports for upper management regarding status of project/program 

  • Lead and directs the work of others

results.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Lab Technician, Hardware 1 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Onsite (Redmond, WA) 

Duration: 3 Months 15 Days  

  

Job Overview: 

The main function of a scanning operator is to prepare and digitize physical books using high‑speed document scanning equipment. This role focuses on safely handling physical materials, operating industrial scanning and cutting equipment, and producing accurate digital files at scale by following established procedures. 

  

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Remove book bindings using industrial cutters and prepare loose pages for scanning 

  • Operate high‑speed document scanners to digitize physical books 

  • Monitor scan quality to identify issues such as double feeds, skew, blurred images, or missing pages 

  • Organize, label, and store digital files according to defined naming and folder conventions 

  • Handle physical materials safely, including disposal of scanned pages and book remnants in accordance with procedures 

  • Maintain a clean, safe, and organized work area while meeting daily throughput and accuracy targets  

  

Job Requirements: 

  • No college degree required; High school diploma or equivalent preferred 

  • Prior experience with manual, operational, or production work is a plus but not required 

  • Prior experience as a Microsoft Corp. contractor 

  • 1-2 years of operating physical equipment safely experience 

  • Visual quality inspection and error detection experience 

  • Basic computer and file handling skills 

  • On‑the‑job training will be provided 

  • Strong attention to detail and ability to maintain accuracy during repetitive tasks 

  • Ability to follow written procedures and safety guidelines consistently 

  • Comfortable performing physical, hands‑on work for extended periods 

  • Basic computer skills for operating scanning software and managing files 

  • Dependable, punctual, and able to work effectively in a production‑style environment 

  • Team‑oriented with a strong focus on quality and consistency 

  

*Salary Range: $34/hr with Medical, Vision, and Dental Benefits

Job Title: Accounting Manager III 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Onsite (Seattle, WA) 

Duration: 4 Months  

   

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Drive program objectives – provide program management and define project plans, manage implementation activities, and develop processes, documentation and communications for program/process rollout and ongoing support 
     
    Designing Accounting month-end close processes with financial transformation from business data inputs 
     
    Playing a key role in automation projects and acting as an accounting SME for our business partners and tech teams

  • Excellent organizational, interpersonal, and communication (written and verbal) skills 

  • Ability to work cross-functionally and with a wide range of people with different skill sets 

  • Experience with Procurement, Supply Chain, and Fixed Assets 

Preferred skills 

  • CPA License 

  • PMP Certificate

Research exp
 
 
 
 
 

Legal Research Librarians

 

Areas of Expertise: Information Services

Team: Research + Intelligence

 

About this role

Harbor is seeking Legal Research Librarians (Contract) to join our specialist teams. These contract positions work with our Research & Information Services team and provide high quality research services and work products for our clients. This is a fully remote/virtual opportunity available on a contractual (1099) basis, nationwide. This role will support specific client engagements on a project-by-project basis.  Depending on the project and client, the number of hours and type of work may slightly differ.

 

Responsibilities:

  • Perform high quality in-depth legal, corporate and other research to support the client’s librarians, attorneys, paralegals and staff using a variety of resources 
  • Monitor and respond to research requests and coordinate this effort with research team members across all locations 
  • Prioritize and respond to requests, analyze and synthesize results, compile answers and communicate research findings in a clear and concise manner 
  • Learn and adopt the specific policies and procedures of the client’s law library department 
  • Provide excellent customer service 

Qualifications:

  • 5 years+ experience in a law firm library in a reference/research position and expertise in legal and corporate research, required 
  • Bachelor's degree is required. Master’s degree in Librarianship or Information Science from an ALA-accredited institution or JD from an ABA-accredited institution is preferred. 
  • Knowledge of standard research resources including, but not limited to, Westlaw, LexisNexis, Bloomberg Law, etc. 
  • Knowledge of a wide variety of general electronic research databases, both fee-based and free, subject-specific databases, and legal analytics tools, required 
  • Excellent communication skills, to effectively deliver and explain research findings to attorneys both verbally and through written summaries and analysis 
  • Self-motivated with the ability to work well independently and to collaborate with client’s team and other contract researchers to effectively prioritize and execute tasks 
  • Reliable internet connection for virtual working 

About Us:

Harbor is the preeminent provider of expert services across strategy, legal technology, operations, and intelligence. Our globally integrated team of 800+ strategists, technologists, and specialists navigate alongside our clients – leading law firms, corporations, and their law departments – to provide essential resources and invaluable insights. Anchored in a rich heritage of deep knowledge, steadfast relationships, and mutual respect, our unwavering dedication lies in shaping the future of the legal industry and fostering enduring partnerships within our community and ecosystem.
results.requisition_description:
Must have - Expereince with being a researcher at a US Law Firm specifically.

 

 

Role: Legal Research Librarian (Contract) – Harbor Global

Hiring Manager: Colleen Cable, Director (https://www.linkedin.com/in/colleencable/)

Work Setup: 100% Remote (either GDM or onshore)

 

Role Overview

  • Contractors are embedded with client law firms to support research needs during staffing gaps (e.g., maternity/paternity leave).
  • Assignments are typically short-term (up to 3 months) with defined start and end dates.
  • They work with small to large law firms.

 

Candidate Profile

  • At least 5+ years of experience
  • Strong familiarity with common legal research tools used by law firms
  • Ability to work independently with little to no training (Training provided tby the client is only to show the candidate how they do things)

 

Must-Haves:

  • U.S. law firm experience is mandatory
  • Background in legal research, library, or intelligence functions

 

Ideal Traits

  • Generalist background(“jack of all trades” in legal research)
  • Highly experienced with law firm research workflows and methodologies
  • Comfortable handling new research requests and problem-solving independently

 

Red Flags

  • Candidates who are attorneys without research/library experience
  • Lack of U.S. law firm research exposure

 

Assessment Process - they agreed to check the candidate's profile first before asking them to do the assessments.

 

Candidates must complete assessments before client interviews:

  • Tools Assessment (around 10 minutes) - Tests familiarity with common legal research platforms.
  • Research Methodology Assessment (30–60 minutes) - Scenario-based research request. It evaluates process, tools used, methodology, and alternative approaches rather than short answers.

 

Additional Notes

  • Contractors are paid hourly.
  • Candidates may remain “benched” until matched with a client (timing varies widely).
  • Clients conduct conflict checks and require paperwork if candidates run their own business or has other employment.
  • Harbor aims to maintain a pool of qualified contractors ready for deployment.
  • Timezone flexibility is important, especially West Coast coverage.

 

Recruiting Challenge

  • Difficult role to fill due less number of candidate pool.

results.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Financial Analyst III 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Onsite (Bellevue, WA) 

Duration: 6 Months  

  

Job Overview: 

We are looking for a dynamic, organized self-starter to join our Finance department as a Financial Analyst in our Seller Support Center. The role will support the Merchant Services Finance team by producing and delivering financial and operational analysis which will allow the Seller Support Contact Centers to work more efficiently and predictably, with the highest level of customer satisfaction. 

  

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Support internal customer groups and controller in all finance and accounting matters including budgeting, forecasting, expense tracking and control 

  • Work with the functional leaders and other finance partners to build scalable financial processes and business metrics 

  • Provide financial and business analysis to site leaders and business partners including cost-saving and process improvement recommendations 

  • Assist in strategic operational planning process addressing identified risks and opportunities for business team 

  • Write systems queries to gather finance data and perform data-mining and analysis using tools including MS Excel, Cognos/Essbase and SQL  

  

Job Requirements: 

  • Bachelor's degree in finance, accounting, business or related field  

  • 5+ years of relevant finance experience  

  • Advanced knowledge of Excel Preferred Qualifications 

  • Advanced analytical skills and an ability to work independently in a fast-paced and rapidly changing environment 

  • Work ethic based on a strong desire to exceed expectations 

  • Strong interpersonal skills including written and oral communication  

  • Demonstrated ability to build and manage financial models for business forecasting, variance analysis, and problem solving  

  • Advanced excel and analysis skills  

  • Experience with Cognos and SQL is a strong plus  

  • Strong interpersonal skills including written and oral communication skills 

  • The ideal candidate will be comfortable with large volumes of complex data and presenting their findings to senior management 

  

*Salary Range: $53/hr with Medical, Vision, and Dental Benefits

results.public_job_desc:
• The role will be responsible for a high volume of tasks including complex calendar management, travel, expenses, visas, effectively prioritizing amidst a flood of information and requests ensuring appropriate level of urgency is given, monitor/track key meetings, priorities and emails and act as proxy when necessary, manage travel and expenses and all administrative support functions for this office.
  • This role is also responsible for managing multiple tasks and action items (documentation, follow up and preparation) for the Director ensuring appropriate and timely action on deliverables.
 
Qualifications:
  • Successful candidates will posses the ability to complete a high volume of tasks and projects with little to no guidance, have impeccable attention to detail, make smart and timely decisions, and react with appropriate levels of urgency to situations and events that require quick response or turnaround.
  • The ideal candidate will also have exceptional verbal and written communications skills, and the ability to switch gears at a moments notice.
  • High levels of integrity and discretion in handling confidential information is a must and a high degree of professionalism in dealing with senior professionals inside and outside the company is required.
  • At least two years of college required, bachelors degree preferred.
  • This position requires 5+ years of experience supporting senior management (VPs, Senior VPs or CEOs) and excellent PC skills, including strong working knowledge of Microsoft Office and Outlook tools.

results.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Program Manager Non Tech 2 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Remote 

Duration: 3 Months  

  

Job Overview: 

The main function of a non-IT program manager is to plan, direct, or coordinate activities in such fields as engineering, research and development, financial systems and product roll-out, etc or any other non-IT based project. 

  

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Coordinate projects, make detailed plans to accomplish goals and direct the integration of technical activities 

  • Present and explain proposals, reports and findings to clients 

  • Recruit employees, assign, direct, and evaluate their work, and oversee the development and maintenance of staff competence 

  • Analyze technology, resource needs and market demand, to plan and assess the feasibility of projects. 

  

Job Requirements: 

  • Bachelor's degree in business administration or in a related field 

  • 2-4 years’ experience required 

  • PMI or PMP certification preferred 

  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills 

  • Problem solving skills, attention to detail and interpersonal skills 

  • Basic ability to work independently and manage one’s time 

  • Basic knowledge of production processes, quality control, costs and other techniques for maximizing the effective manufacture and distribution of goods 

  • Basic knowledge of business and management principles involved in strategic planning, resource allocation, and production methods 

  • Basic knowledge of computer software, such as MS Word, MS PowerPoint, MS Project, Visio etc. 
     

  

*Salary Range: $9, 467/mo with Medical, Vision, and Dental Benefits

results.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Usability Researcher 2 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Onsite (Redmond, WA) 

Duration: 3 Months 8 Days  

  

Job Overview: 

We are looking for a UX Researcher with the ability to conduct a range of qualitative and quantitative research methods impacting both product direction and detailed experience design. You will work with and receive mentorship from a supervising Senior UX Researcher. You will be joining 20+ talented UX researchers whose charter is to influence product direction, determine what to build, and design end-to-end experiences for all developers that are useful, usable, and desirable. In this role, you will work in a highly collaborative environment with engineering, design, product management, and business teams across the division to help make customer-driven decisions. Your work will happen in the context of a lean or agile development environment supporting the release of cadence of teams as well as helping define the strategic direction of products. You will have access to a state-of-the-art, modern research lab facility equipped for a variety of research types located in a central hub area on campus. You will use remote tools incorporating the latest technology to collaborate with a distributed team and to execute research on a global customer base. 

  

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Design and apply a range of qualitative and quantitative research methods to all product stages from conception to release, including lab-based usability tests, interviews, surveys, focus groups, and field research 

  • Collaborate closely with engineering, design, product management, and business partners during all steps of research 

  • Translate research findings into actionable recommendations and deliver them in compelling ways 

  • Triangulate multiple data sources into integrated insights 

  • Communicate and evangelize research results to engineering and business teams 

  • Evangelize UX research as an essential aspect of product development 

  • Coach other roles on UX research best practices and methods 

  

Job Requirements: 

  • A degree in human-computer interaction, cognitive psychology, behavioral science, or related field or equivalent experience 

  • Prior experience as a Microsoft Corp. contractor

  • Minimum 4 years of experience with both Qualitative and Quantitative research methods 

  • Minimum 4 years of experience with a technical background/experience in a technical industry 

  • A Portfolio/previous work samples (The ideal portfolio shows off the candidate’s previous work and describes the candidate’s impact and role on the project) 

  • Thorough knowledge and experience of a range of UX research approaches and methodologies 

  • Strong analytical and synthesis skills with a proven ability to turn research results into product insights 

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to influence a range of audiences 

  • Excellent project management skills, including the ability to manage multiple projects concurrently 

  • The ability to work independently and be flexible to rapidly changing schedules and demands 

  • The ability to get up to speed with a new area quickly 

  • Familiarity with modern developer platforms and ecosystems and/or strong aptitude for learning new technologies 

  • Value diversity and inclusivity in the workplace 

  

*Salary Range: $55/hr with Medical, Vision, and Dental Benefits
results.requisition_description:

  • Excellent project management skills, including the ability to manage multiple projects concurrently 

  • The ability to work independently and be flexible to rapidly changing schedules and demands 

  • The ability to get up to speed with a new area quickly 

  • Familiarity with modern developer platforms and ecosystems and/or strong aptitude for learning new technologies 

  • Value diversity and inclusivity in the workplace 

  

*Salary Range: $55/hr with Medical, Vision, and Dental Benefits
results.skills: Minimum 4 years experience with a technical background/experience in a technical industry, Minimum 4 years experience with both Qualitative and Quantitative research methods

results.public_job_desc:
Job Title: Fraud Operations Specialist I 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Hybrid (Overland Park, KS) 

Duration: 6 Months  

  

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Conduct in depth analysis and/or investigations to identify and manage fraudulent activities 

  • Responsible for customer authentication, fraud investigations and/or compliance with fraud policies in all business channels 

  • Analyze relevant data to confirm or clear fraud, validate customer ID authentication and violations of fraud polices that could adversely impact the company 

  • Evaluate fraud trends related to fraudulent orders and identity theft 

  • Perform research and draw conclusions 

  • Research and/or notify appropriate internal staff for customer verification or legal action 

  • Participate in establishment and implementation of policies and procedures designed to identify and deter fraudulent usage 

  • Investigate and respond to fraud escalations. Establishes and improves fraud-related process 

  • Identify issues and recommend solutions on team projects  

  • Review, identify, and deter fraudulent activity 

  • Take inbound calls and referrals from victims of fraudulent activities 

  • Assess the available information via multiple internal and external tools to identify the situation and provide guidance to resolve issue 

  

Job Requirements: 

  • High School diploma or equivalent   

  • At least 1 year experience operating a computer in a Windows based environment required 

  • Must have excellent conversational skills in English 

  • Experience navigating multiple computer screens and programs while interacting with customers on the phone 

  • 1 year experience in fraud, customer service or a call center environment Technology  

  • Phone skills, typing skills, and good communication skills  

  • Customer service and call center skills  

  • Good tone and communication skills as wells as listening skills  

  

*Salary Range: $19/hr with Medical, Vision, and Dental Benefits

results.public_job_desc: Performs a variety of administrative functions. Schedules appointments, gives information to callers, and takes dictation. Composes memos, transcribes notes, and researches and creates presentations. Generates reports, handles multiple projects, and prepares and monitors invoices and expense reports. May assist with compiling and developing the annual budget. Requires a high school diploma with 2-4 years of experience in the field or in a related area. Familiar with standard concepts, practices, and procedures within a particular field. Relies on experience and judgment to plan and accomplish goals. Performs a variety of tasks. Works under general supervision. A certain degree of creativity and latitude is required. Typically reports to a supervisor or manager.

Job Title: Administrative Support II 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Onsite (Bellevue, WA) 

Duration: 6 Months  

   

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Note-taking 

  • Recycle space had to rebuild the recycle program from scratch to replace a vendor 

  • With new program they need to invoice the vendor that didn’t have to be done before 

  • Submit purchase orders 

  • Preapprove spend, manage invoices for several different cost centers/locations 

  

Job Requirements: 

  • Utilizing AI/familiarity with AI tools 

  • Prior Amazon experience 

  • Ability to have an impact on second life of an item 

  • Secondhand inventory is a cool aspect to be a part of 

  • Work with a ton of vendors 

  • So interesting to see the last parts of the recycling steps 

  

*Salary Range: $21/hr with Medical, Vision, and Dental Benefits

results.public_job_desc:
Job Title: HR Service Desk 

Job Type: Contract 

Site Type: Onsite (Overland Park, KS) 

Duration: 4 Months  

  

Job Overview: 

The myHR team members are the first point of contact for all HR questions, including Payroll, Manager Self-Service, Employee Data, New Hire Onboarding, Learning, Timekeeping, Performance Management processes and Benefit inquiries. This role serves as the primary employee contact utilizing a knowledge base and case management tool along with a multi-line phone, email, chat, and ticketing system. The position provides functional and technical support for managers and employees with resolutions that are timely and accurate to help enhance the Employee Experience, while educating employees along the way to promote employee self-service. It requires customer focus skills in order to act as the customer advocate and help alleviate pain points. The role should demonstrate a high level of HR policy and program expertise and will have autonomy to educate on HR policy and procedure. The role will exercise structured decision-making responsibilities when evaluating employee and manager inquiries and when educating employees on appropriate HR policy and procedures. 

  

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Answer and resolve a high volume of HR related inquiries (i.e. payroll, benefits, timekeeping, and HR policies) via emails, phones, tickets, and chat system to ensure first time resolution 

  • Troubleshoot and provide technical support to exceed service levels while referring employees to appropriate departments as needed 

  • Communicate the status of escalated issues to the supervisor/management on a timely basis 

  • Troubleshoot and resolve issues that might prevent timely processing or result in data integrity issues 

  • Acquire and maintain knowledge of relevant HR programs, processes, technology, and policies to provide accurate problem diagnosis for low to critical HR issues in a timely and efficient manner 

  • Identify process improvement opportunities that enhance the quality and efficiency of the HR Services team and the Employee Experience 

  • Create and maintain documentation for the existing knowledge base tool (Internal Portal that houses HR Services information) 
      

 Job Requirements: 

  •  1-2 years of call center experience 

  • ServiceNow and Workday experience 

  

*Salary Range: $26/hr with Medical, Vision, and Dental Benefits

results.public_job_desc:
Intake call notes:
 
About the role:
  • The role has been open since September, and they’ve interviewed multiple candidates already, but have no offers yet (didn’t really answer the question if they have anyone in the mix -seemed like they’re back to square one and letting agencies work on it since their internal team has been working on it for 6 months now)
  • The role is urgent since the person backfilling is retiring in March, but they have been working on this role since September but still unable to hire anyone
  • Decline reasons:
  • Lengthy resumes (8pages long), SQL expertise and depth, HA/DR experience, they need someone who can speak to their experience
  • Longevity (not someone who is jumping after 2-3 years)
  • Can you tell us a little about the role? What are your top three must-haves and nice-to-haves?
    Top things:
    • 8 years plus-SQL server expert -In-depth knowledge of SQL (versions they use SQL 19, 22, 25 Attunity)
    • Longevity 2-3 years and Progression
    • HA/DR experience, they need someone who can really speak to their technical expertise and the projects they worked with
    • Project leadership, SME/Go to person
Preference: Ansible, MySQL
  • Can you tell us about the team and structure?
    • HM- Harshal is the direct supervisor, but is unable to attend the meeting. I asked about his personality, and they said – (he’s a reserved, personable, approachable person, and people are not afraid to come to him)
    • 8-person team -tight-knit, close collaboration and relationships with the team – tenured team, but they will have interns coming in March
  • I saw the salary is $115k - $150k, is their room on top candidates, or do you not want to see candidates over $150k?
    • The ideal salary for the role is $150K, though they may be open to a higher range for candidates with strong experience.
    • Regarding location, they are open to hiring candidates based in either Illinois (IL) or Wisconsin (WI), with a preference for Wisconsin. They are also willing to consider candidates who can initially report onsite in their Wisconsin office a few days per week and later transition to Illinois.
  • What is your interview process?
    • Initial interview with Michael, 2 interviews with HM, technical interview, and onsite interviews.
  • Do you have any questions for us about our process that we didn’t cover?
  • Sanjeev mentioned that it is critical that they can be flexible on weekends for troubleshooting –rotation happens often- every 6-7 weeks
Wrap up:
  • Their deal candidate is a hands-on SQL Server expert with strong HA/DR and replication experience, capable of mentoring a small team, collaborating with development, and optimizing business-critical databases.
  • Jo will schedule a weekly touchbase with the HM
  • Focus on open-ended questions to really qualify candidates- sample questions
  • Can you tell me about the projects you worked on and your role in them?
  • Tell us about the projects you led or where you mentored Junior Devs
  • Can you walk me through your experience administering SQL Server environments, particularly your involvement with high availability and disaster recovery solutions?”
